steps.title: Fastest path: try the choices
steps.content: Because the choices give possible solutions, plug them into the equation instead of solving from scratch. The only choice that satisfies the equation is D, $-6+2\sqrt{14}$.

steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content: This is a quadratic equation, so the goal is to find its roots. The quadratic formula is a direct way to solve it when simple factoring is not the focus. Apply the quadratic formula to $w^2+12w-20=0$ with $a=1, b=12, c=-20$: $$w=\frac{-12\pm\sqrt{144+80}}{2}=\frac{-12\pm\sqrt{224}}{2}.$$ Since $\sqrt{224}=4\sqrt{14}$, $w=-6\pm 2\sqrt{14}$.

steps.title: Identify the matching solution
steps.content: The two solutions are $-6+2\sqrt{14}$ and $-6-2\sqrt{14}$. D is correct.
